vps-line-verification-guide.title: Before You Buy a VPS: Verify Routes with Nationwide Carrier Maps and Spot Fake CN2 Claims (SpeedCE Playbook)
vps-line-verification-guide.hook: In HostLoc threads, buyers say "my ping is 28ms" and sellers claim "three-carrier direct"—a week later Mobile users complain. Sample size is 1, and the sample is you. Nationwide three-carrier maps are the only reliable acceptance standard.
vps-line-verification-guide.keywords: VPS speed test,CN2 GIA verification,three-carrier testing,HostLoc acceptance,SpeedCE

cdn-deployment-speed-test-guide.title: CDN Deployment Playbook: Multi-Node Speed Test Acceptance Before, During, and After Cutover
cdn-deployment-speed-test-guide.hook: CDN enabled but some users can't open? The issue is usually not "is CDN on" but wrong acceptance method. Comparative testing—CDN hostname vs origin—is the golden rule of CDN ops.

global-deployment-checklist.title: Global Website Launch Acceptance: Complete Checklist from China Nodes to Worldwide Probes
global-deployment-checklist.hook: Your .com opens instantly in Shanghai; German customers see spinning wheels—the test perspective is wrong. Global launch needs probes in target markets; China and global views are both mandatory.

dns-troubleshooting-guide.title: Complete DNS Troubleshooting Guide: When "Some Regions Can't Open" After Migration or CDN Switch
dns-troubleshooting-guide.hook: DNS changed and works instantly for you; colleague in Xinjiang still sees old IP—not their PC, but resolver chains out of sync by geography and carrier. DNS causes half of "regional access" tickets yet gets blamed on "user network."

ssl-certificate-troubleshooting.title: SSL Certificate Expiry and Misconfiguration: 10-Minute Playbook When Users See "Connection Not Secure"
ssl-certificate-troubleshooting.hook: Certificate issues torture ops: your browser works, users mass-report "your connection is not private." Local HSTS cache, clicking through warnings, test whitelists—all fool you.

nginx-reverse-proxy-troubleshooting.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy Troubleshooting: 8 Typical Config Errors When Main Site Is Green but API Is Red
nginx-reverse-proxy-troubleshooting.hook: Nginx fronts countless sites. One wrong server_name or missing proxy_pass means homepage works, APIs die. Postman works locally; nationwide users fail.
terms: Separate server block per public domain
One dead backend can drag all
Every HTTPS server block needs pair

website-migration-guide.title: Complete Website Migration Handbook: 72-Hour Speed Test Acceptance for DNS, Origin, and CDN
website-migration-guide.hook: Migration is among the most stressful changes. SSH on new box looks perfect, but DNS propagates globally and CDN may still point at old origin—multi-node speed tests are the objective witness.
terms: Check CDN origin IP after move

intermittent-fault-diagnosis.title: Intermittent Website Fault Diagnosis: Scientific Checkpointing for "Sometimes Slow, Sometimes Fine"
intermittent-fault-diagnosis.hook: Intermittent faults are ops nightmares: always fine when you test, broken when users complain. Single probes aren't enough—schedule repeated tests and watch success rate and latency curves.

subdomain-troubleshooting.title: Complete Subdomain Troubleshooting: 8 Independent Reasons When Main Site Works but API Fails
subdomain-troubleshooting.hook: www.example.com and api.example.com have separate DNS, certs, Nginx, and CDN configs. Main green ≠ subdomain green—every public subdomain deserves its own SpeedCE map.
terms: Only www configured is common pitfall

api-availability-guide.title: API Availability Testing Guide: Why Postman Works but Nationwide Users Don't
api-availability-guide.hook: API failures surface last: frontend cache still serves pages; apps hit APIs and fail immediately. SpeedCE HTTPS probes from nationwide nodes are step one of network acceptance.
terms: Network green can still CORS fail

http-https-redirect-issues.title: HTTP/HTTPS Redirect Failures: Forced HTTPS, Redirect Loops, and Mixed Content Triage
http-https-redirect-issues.hook: 301 loops, http and https on different machines, page assets still on http—users see varied symptoms. SpeedCE HTTP/HTTPS dual-mode comparison narrows scope fast.

firewall-security-group-checklist.title: Cloud Security Group Acceptance: Four Checks When the Nationwide Map Is Mostly Red
firewall-security-group-checklist.hook: Classic beginner mistake: SSH works, website all red nationwide—security group allows 22 but not 443. Before blaming routes, CDN, or DNS, confirm port layer with SpeedCE.

502-503-upstream-errors.title: 502/503 and Origin Overload: When CDN Is Green but Origin Is Red
502-503-upstream-errors.hook: 502 means gateway got bad response; 503 means temporarily unavailable. Users on CDN see 502—could be edge, but more often origin can't handle load—comparative test decides.

dns-propagation-slow.title: Slow DNS Propagation: TTL, Carrier Cache, and Regional Resolver Differences
dns-propagation-slow.hook: DNS doesn't change worldwide at once. TTL=86400 means up to 24 hours worst case. SpeedCE every 10 minutes—random fading vs stubborn fixed provinces.

regional-access-failure.title: Only Some Regions Can't Open? Use Maps to Pin Province, Carrier, and Next Steps
regional-access-failure.hook: "Only Xinjiang" or "only Mobile"—averages and success rates don't help. Maps are the language of regional faults. SpeedCE China map is built for this.

mobile-network-issues.title: Mobile Network Access Issues: Why Mobile Users Complain the Most
mobile-network-issues.hook: China Mobile users exceed 50%, but many "optimized routes" only help Telecom/Unicom. Skip Mobile map = ignore half your users.

single-carrier-fault.title: Single-Carrier Fault: When One of Telecom/Unicom/Mobile Is All Red
single-carrier-fault.hook: Split three carriers—one all red shrinks fault domain by 66%. Route issue, CDN per-carrier config, or carrier DNS? Comparative tests point the way.

peak-hour-slowdown.title: Peak-Hour Slowdown: Retest Strategy When Afternoon Is Green but Evening Turns Red
peak-hour-slowdown.hook: Bandwidth, international egress, attack traffic—peak hours are the truth mirror. Sellers show test IP in afternoon; you retest 20:00-22:00 on SpeedCE.

ddos-attack-detection.title: Using Multi-Node Speed Tests During Attacks to Assess Impact
ddos-attack-detection.hook: Speed tests don't replace DDoS protection, but when nationwide nodes turn red and latency spikes together, plus traffic graphs, you confirm attack vs misconfiguration faster.

ipv6-troubleshooting.title: IPv6 Dual-Stack Acceptance: AAAA Records, Firewall, and CDN Complete Check
ipv6-troubleshooting.hook: IPv4 all green doesn't mean IPv6 is fine. Dual-stack sites need separate IPv4 and IPv6 target tests.

mixed-content-https.title: Mixed Content and HTTPS: Network Green but Browser Still "Not Secure"
mixed-content-https.hook: SpeedCE tests site reachability; mixed content is http:// assets on HTTPS pages. Clear division—don't waste time on network layer for browser-only issues.

cors-vs-network-testing.title: CORS Errors vs Network Failure: Two Layers Developers Must Separate
cors-vs-network-testing.hook: Map all green + browser CORS error—congrats, network works; server headers missing. SpeedCE rules out network, then check Access-Control-Allow-Origin.

wechat-qq-access-guide.title: WeChat/QQ Can't Open: Standard Split Between Network Layer and Compliance
wechat-qq-access-guide.hook: Browser works, WeChat doesn't—not always server fault. SpeedCE excludes network layer; blocking/ICP/content need BOCE and compliance tools.

waf-false-positive-guide.title: WAF False Positives and Probe Anomalies: Are Sporadic Red Dots a Block?
waf-false-positive-guide.hook: WAF, CC protection, geo block—may only hit some probe IPs, showing sporadic red not whole-province red.

database-not-network-guide.title: Database Killing the Site: Network Green but Page Timeout—Application Layer Triage
database-not-network-guide.hook: SpeedCE green + page timeout—network is fine; check MySQL slow queries and connection pool exhaustion. Network first, application second—order matters.

load-balancer-health-check.title: Load Balancer and Health Checks: Half Green Half Red Architecture Patterns
load-balancer-health-check.hook: Multiple backends, one down—DNS round robin or bad LB config feels like "sometimes works." Multi-node probes + repeated tests confirm.

cache-poisoning-stale.title: Stale Cache: CDN/Browser Cache vs Network Layer Comparative Troubleshooting
cache-poisoning-stale.hook: You fixed the server; users still see old page—likely cache. SpeedCE probes live network response; add random query or test API to bypass cache.

third-party-script-failure.title: Third-Party Scripts Breaking Pages: Main Domain Green but Features Broken
third-party-script-failure.hook: Payment, analytics, chat widgets on third-party domains—main site green doesn't mean checkout works. List every critical third-party domain in SpeedCE patrol.

hong-kong-vps-guide.title: Hong Kong VPS Route Selection and Acceptance: Personal Sites, E-Commerce, and API Scenarios
hong-kong-vps-guide.hook: Hong Kong is familiar to Chinese users: low latency, no ICP for many cases, fair bandwidth pricing. But CN2, CMI, BGP claims mix—nationwide three-carrier maps are the only honest acceptance.

japan-vps-guide.title: Japan VPS for Which Workloads: Tokyo/Osaka DCs and Three-Carrier Return Route Acceptance
japan-vps-guide.hook: Japan VPS is cheap with good bandwidth and streaming-friendly, but return routes to China vary. Test seller IP on SpeedCE before pay—Mobile map is veto power.

us-vps-china-access.title: US VPS Three-Carrier Return Route Guide: West Coast DC Verification and Mobile Users
us-vps-china-access.hook: US VPS is cheap and roomy, but return path is long. Telecom may be OK; Mobile often disaster. Don't trust "LA 150ms"—that's your local ping, not a nationwide map.

singapore-vps-guide.title: Singapore VPS Acceptance: Southeast Asia Hub and Dual China/Global Map Testing
singapore-vps-guide.hook: Singapore is Asia-Pacific hub—for China return and Southeast Asia traffic. Dual view: China nodes for return, global for SEA coverage.

cn2-gt-vs-gia.title: CN2 GT vs CN2 GIA: Speed Test Verification Behind Marketing Claims
cn2-gt-vs-gia.hook: Two letters difference, one tier of experience. GT may congest at peak; GIA costs more but stable. Trust three-carrier maps + peak retest, not copy.

bgp-line-verification.title: Real vs Fake BGP: Three-Carrier Balance Is the Acceptance Standard
bgp-line-verification.hook: True BGP: Telecom, Unicom, Mobile all usable. Fake BGP: Telecom green, Mobile red. SpeedCE three-carrier split is the mirror.

cmi-mobile-line-guide.title: CMI Mobile-Optimized Route Acceptance: Mobile Users Are Half Your Audience
cmi-mobile-line-guide.hook: Skip Mobile map = abandon half your users. Whether CMI/CMIN2 is real optimization—maps decide.

vps-refund-period-checklist.title: VPS 7-Day Refund Acceptance Checklist: Screenshots, Three Carriers, Peak Evidence
vps-refund-period-checklist.hook: Refunds need evidence: three-carrier screenshots + success rates + peak comparison—100× better than forum "feels slow."

cloud-security-group-vps.title: First Step on New Cloud Server: Security Group and Firewall Before Route Talk
cloud-security-group-vps.hook: Nationwide red—don't refund yet—maybe 443 closed. SpeedCE HTTPS red + SSH works = security group issue.

ping-blocked-not-bad.title: ICMP Blocked ≠ Bad Route: Reading PING Red with HTTPS Green
ping-blocked-not-bad.hook: Newbies panic on ping timeout. Cloud default ICMP block is normal. Acceptance standard: HTTPS success ≥ 90%.

off-peak-vs-peak-vps.title: Off-Peak vs Peak VPS Testing: Why Good Routes Need Two Tests
off-peak-vs-peak-vps.hook: Afternoon test IP looks great; peak hour tells truth. Quality routes must pass both windows.

home-broadband-vs-datacenter.title: Home Broadband vs Nationwide Nodes: Why Your Fast Ping Doesn't Mean Users Are Fast
home-broadband-vs-datacenter.hook: Your home ping is one sample on one carrier in one province. Nationwide probe nodes represent real user distribution.

vps-with-cdn-comparison.title: VPS Before and After CDN: Map Comparison for CDN Go/No-Go Decisions
vps-with-cdn-comparison.hook: Data beats opinions: same target with and without CDN on three-carrier maps—does latency and success improve enough to justify cost?

used-ip-segment-check.title: Used IP Segment Warning Signs: Blocked or Flagged IPs on Nationwide Maps
used-ip-segment-check.hook: Cheap recycled IPs may be blocked or blacklisted. China all red + global green is a classic warning pattern before purchase.

datacenter-failover-verify.title: Post-DC-Failover Emergency Verification: 24-Hour SpeedCE Checkpoint SOP
datacenter-failover-verify.hook: After emergency hardware swap, don't assume parity—run scheduled SpeedCE checkpoints for 24 hours on three carriers.

europe-vps-china-guide.title: Europe VPS Return Routes to China: Real Experience from DE/FR/NL DCs
europe-vps-china-guide.hook: European VPS looks cheap; return to China is the hard part. Map acceptance from China nodes is mandatory for domestic users.

korea-vps-guide.title: Korea VPS Route Review: Close to China ≠ All Three Carriers Good
korea-vps-guide.hook: Geographic proximity doesn't guarantee Telecom/Unicom/Mobile quality. Test maps, especially Mobile, before commit.

taiwan-vps-guide.title: Taiwan VPS Acceptance: Latency Advantages vs Marketing Claims
taiwan-vps-guide.hook: Taiwan often shows low latency, but carrier routing and political/network factors need map verification—not brochure latency.

budget-vps-trap-guide.title: Ultra-Low-Price VPS Traps: Six Danger Signals Visible on Maps
budget-vps-trap-guide.hook: Too cheap often means oversold routes, blocked IPs, or Mobile disaster. Maps surface six red flags before you pay.

dedicated-vs-vps-line.title: Dedicated Server vs VPS Route Acceptance: IP Segment, Neighbors, and Test Notes
dedicated-vs-vps-line.hook: Dedicated IPs behave differently from shared VPS neighbors. Acceptance still ends on three-carrier maps with neighbor noise in mind.

cloudflare-china-access.title: Cloudflare Orange Cloud and China Access: Complete Acceptance Handbook
cloudflare-china-access.hook: Cloudflare on doesn't mean China is fast. Orange cloud changes path—accept with China node maps and origin comparison.

aliyun-cdn-acceptance.title: Alibaba Cloud CDN Acceptance: Origin, Certificate, Warmup, and Three-Carrier Checks
aliyun-cdn-acceptance.hook: Aliyun CDN has many knobs—origin, HTTPS cert, cache rules. Three-carrier SpeedCE maps are the final acceptance gate.

tencent-cdn-acceptance.title: Tencent Cloud CDN Acceptance: Static vs Full-Site Acceleration and Speed Test Points
tencent-cdn-acceptance.hook: Static CDN vs DCDN behave differently on acceptance. Map test each product mode against origin.

cdn-cache-vs-speed-test.title: CDN Cache vs Active Probing: Why First Request Slow, Second Fast
cdn-cache-vs-speed-test.hook: Probes hit cold cache differently than users. Understand cache layer when interpreting first vs repeat SpeedCE results.

cdn-origin-failure.title: CDN Origin Failure Complete Troubleshooting: Edge, Timeout, and Origin Comparison
cdn-origin-failure.hook: CDN red often traces to origin, not edge. Always pair CDN hostname map with origin IP map.

multi-cdn-comparison.title: Multi-CDN Trial Map Comparison: Scientific Vendor Selection on Same Domain
multi-cdn-comparison.hook: Run parallel trials—same content, different CDN—compare three-carrier maps during trial window before annual contract.

static-cdn-split.title: Static Asset CDN Split Acceptance: Independent Speed Test for js/css Domains
static-cdn-split.hook: js.example.com and www.example.com are separate DNS/CDN/certs. Test every public static domain on the checklist.

dcdn-vs-cdn.title: DCDN vs Standard CDN: Acceptance Standards and SpeedCE Comparative Method
dcdn-vs-cdn.hook: Full-site acceleration vs static CDN—different failure modes. Accept each with comparative origin tests.

cdn-cert-vs-origin.title: CDN Certificate vs Origin Certificate: Both Sides Must Be Green
cdn-cert-vs-origin.hook: Edge cert can be fine while origin TLS breaks backhaul—or reverse. Test CDN hostname and origin separately.

overseas-cdn-china-pack.title: Overseas CDN China Acceleration Pack: When Global Green but China Slow
overseas-cdn-china-pack.hook: Global map green, China red on overseas CDN is common. Decide if China pack, domestic CDN, or separate .cn is needed.

cdn-cutover-72h.title: CDN Cutover 72-Hour Monitoring: Hour-by-Hour from T+0 to T+72
cdn-cutover-72h.hook: CDN switch isn't done at DNS flip. Hourly SpeedCE checkpoints for 72 hours catch stubborn provincial cache and node gaps.

free-cdn-enough.title: Is Free CDN Enough? Personal Site Decisions with Nationwide Map Data
free-cdn-enough.hook: Free tiers have limits—nodes, HTTPS, China path. Use map data to decide if free CDN meets your audience or upgrade is mandatory.

huawei-baidu-cdn-guide.title: Huawei Cloud / Baidu Cloud CDN Acceptance and Three-Carrier Map Standards
huawei-baidu-cdn-guide.hook: Domestic cloud CDNs need same rigorous three-carrier acceptance as any vendor—console green isn't user green.

cdn-websocket-stream.title: CDN WebSocket/Live Stream Reachability: Acceptance Boundaries
cdn-websocket-stream.hook: SpeedCE HTTPS probes don't equal WebSocket or RTMP health. Know tool boundaries; supplement with stream-specific checks.

edge-function-troubleshoot.title: Edge Functions/Workers Failures: Main Domain Green but Rules Not Applied
edge-function-troubleshoot.hook: Workers and edge rules fail independently of origin. Test both default route and rule-triggered paths.

saas-global-launch.title: Global SaaS Launch Acceptance: Target Market Success Rate Playbook
saas-global-launch.hook: Launch where customers are—not where your team is. Target-country nodes must hit ≥95% before marketing spend.

cross-border-ecommerce.title: Cross-Border E-Commerce Speed Test Guide: Shopify/WooCommerce and Pre-Sale Acceptance
cross-border-ecommerce.hook: Black Friday doesn't forgive slow checkout. Pre-sale map acceptance on payment and storefront domains in target countries.

europe-us-slow-fix.title: EU/US Users Slow: Origin, CDN, and DC Location Triangle
europe-us-slow-fix.hook: Fixing EU slowness isn't one knob—origin location, CDN PoPs, and database region interact. Map each leg.

southeast-asia-nodes.title: Southeast Asia Market Node Acceptance: SG/MY/TH/ID/PH per-Country Targets
southeast-asia-nodes.hook: SEA isn't one market—accept per country with global nodes focused on each target, not just Singapore hub.

global-team-china-admin.title: Global Team Accessing China Admin: Dual-Map Collaboration and Acceleration Options
global-team-china-admin.hook: Engineers abroad need China admin; China team needs global SaaS—dual maps expose both directions and guide acceleration SKU choice.

dual-site-cn-com.title: Dual .cn and .com Strategy: Split Domain Speed Tests and Compliance Roles
dual-site-cn-com.hook: .cn and .com often serve different audiences and compliance regimes—test and accept each domain independently.

geodns-verification.title: GeoDNS Verification: Speed Testing When Resolvers Return Different IPs by Region
geodns-verification.hook: GeoDNS means Beijing and Berlin resolve differently—probe from multiple regions and compare resolved targets.

cross-border-sale-prep.title: Cross-Border Sale Prep: Black Friday/Christmas Speed Test Checklist
cross-border-sale-prep.hook: Holiday traffic spikes expose weak CDN and payment domains. T-7 to T+0 schedule with SpeedCE on all conversion paths.

overseas-live-streaming.title: Overseas Live Streaming and Video Conference Node Selection: Latency-Sensitive Map Standards
overseas-live-streaming.hook: Streaming needs low jitter to target viewers—map latency percentiles in viewer countries, not just average.

game-server-global.title: Game Global Server Placement: Player Distribution vs Global PING Maps
game-server-global.hook: Place shards where players are—overlay player geo data with SpeedCE global ping maps before launch.

stripe-payment-domain-check.title: Global Payment Domain Checks: Payment Page and Callback URL Independent Probes
stripe-payment-domain-check.hook: Stripe and payment pages live on separate domains—checkout can fail while marketing site is green. Test payment URLs per country.

multilingual-site-delivery.title: Multilingual Global Delivery: hreflang and Per-Locale Reachability Acceptance
multilingual-site-delivery.hook: Each locale path or subdomain may hit different CDN rules—accept every public locale URL from target regions.

china-blocked-overseas-ok.title: Global Green, China Red: Standard Workflow for Blocking and Compliance Questions
china-blocked-overseas-ok.hook: Overseas site working worldwide but not China may be GFW, ICP, or routing—not always "fix the server." Structured decision tree.

personal-blog-launch.title: Personal Blog Launch Acceptance: Hexo/Hugo/WordPress Universal Speed Checklist
personal-blog-launch.hook: Even small blogs deserve nationwide HTTPS acceptance before you share the link—especially on Mobile carriers.

wordpress-troubleshooting.title: WordPress Troubleshooting: White Screen, 502, and Plugin Conflicts—Network Layer First
wordpress-troubleshooting.hook: WP issues masquerade as network faults. SpeedCE green means shift to PHP/plugins; red means fix infra first.

ecommerce-sale-prep.title: E-Commerce 618/Double-11 Prep: Multi-Node Speed Test Battle Plan
ecommerce-sale-prep.hook: Domestic mega-sales need three-carrier maps on storefront, API, payment, and static domains—T-7 through T+0.

online-education-platform.title: Online Education Platform Pre-Class Acceptance: Video, Live, and API Domains
online-education-platform.hook: Class start failures damage refunds and reputation—accept video CDN, live push/pull, and API on three carriers before semester.

corporate-website-sla.title: Corporate Website SLA: Reporting Availability with Success Rate Data to Management
corporate-website-sla.hook: Executives want numbers—not "seems fine." Monthly SpeedCE success rates by carrier become SLA evidence.

miniprogram-backend-api.title: Mini Program Backend API Nationwide Acceptance: Legal Domain, ICP, and Mobile Network
miniprogram-backend-api.hook: WeChat mini programs require reachable APIs on allowed domains—Mobile network acceptance is non-negotiable in China.

mobile-app-api-domain.title: Mobile App API Domain Monitoring: iOS/Android Mismatch Network Troubleshooting
mobile-app-api-domain.hook: When iOS and Android users disagree, compare API domain maps on Mobile vs Telecom and check DNS split.

game-private-server-ping.title: Private Game Server Community: Building Trust with Nationwide PING Maps
game-private-server-ping.hook: Player trust grows when admins post SpeedCE ping maps—not verbal "low ping." Transparency reduces churn accusations.

forum-community-site.title: Forum Community Nationwide Reachability: Discuz/Flarum Three-Carrier Acceptance
forum-community-site.hook: Forums spike on controversy and events—baseline three-carrier acceptance prevents "only my province can't post" crises.

download-site-bandwidth.title: Download Site Reachability vs Bandwidth: Probing vs Download Speed Division
download-site-bandwidth.hook: SpeedCE proves reachability; large file throughput needs download tests. Both matter for mirror sites.

government-site-standard.title: Government/Public Sector Sites: Nationwide Availability and IPv6 Dual-Stack Standards
government-site-standard.hook: Public sites face stricter availability and IPv6 mandates—documented three-carrier and dual-stack acceptance.

fintech-medical-compliance.title: Fintech/Medical Website Network Baseline: HTTPS, Certificates, and Multi-Site Acceptance
fintech-medical-compliance.hook: Regulated industries need auditable network baselines—HTTPS success, cert validity, and DR site maps on record.

saas-b2b-demo-environment.title: B2B SaaS Demo Environment: Map Acceptance in Prospect Regions
saas-b2b-demo-environment.hook: Demo URLs that fail in prospect's province kill deals—probe demo domains from their country/region before calls.

news-media-peak-traffic.title: News Media Peak Traffic: Nationwide Checkpoint SOP Before Breaking Stories
news-media-peak-traffic.hook: Viral articles spike in minutes—pre-publish SpeedCE checkpoint on origin and CDN prevents national embarrassment.

how-to-read-speed-map.title: How to Read Speed Test Maps: Green/Red/Gray, Latency, and Success Rate Explained
how-to-read-speed-map.hook: Maps intimidate newcomers—this guide decodes every color and metric so one screenshot tells a story.

tri-network-method.title: Three-Carrier Split Testing Complete Guide: Why Telecom, Unicom, Mobile Must Be Separate
tri-network-method.hook: Averaging three carriers hides Mobile disasters. Split maps are mandatory for China audience acceptance.

ab-comparison-method.title: A/B Comparative Speed Testing: CDN vs Origin, Before/After Migration, Competitors
ab-comparison-method.hook: Single-target tests lie; pairs reveal truth. Systematic A/B methodology for every major infra decision.

screenshot-archive-sop.title: Speed Test Screenshot Archive SOP: Standards for Tickets, Forums, and Incident Reports
screenshot-archive-sop.hook: Screenshots without metadata are useless. Filename, time, protocol, target—archive SOP for defensible evidence.

customer-support-scripts.title: Customer Support Speed Test Scripts: 20+ Professional Replies for "Can't Open"
customer-support-scripts.hook: Stop saying "works for me." Copy-paste scripts with map data, scope, and next steps for support teams.

pre-launch-30-checklist.title: Pre-Launch 30-Item Checklist: Including 8 Mandatory Multi-Node Speed Tests
pre-launch-30-checklist.hook: Launch gates prevent Friday night disasters—eight SpeedCE items among thirty before go-live.

monthly-inspection-sop.title: Monthly Website Inspection SOP: 15 Minutes Personal, 1 Hour Enterprise
monthly-inspection-sop.hook: Monthly rhythm catches drift before users do—scaled SOP for personal blogs vs enterprise properties.

quarterly-infra-review.title: Quarterly Infrastructure Review: Map Trends, Degradation, and Upgrade Decisions
quarterly-infra-review.hook: Quarterly side-by-side maps reveal slow degradation—upgrade CDN or routes before success rate cliff.

protocol-selection-guide.title: SpeedCE Tool Selection Guide: HTTP, HTTPS, PING, TCPing, DNS & Traceroute
protocol-selection-guide.hook: SpeedCE's tool dropdown now bundles six probes. Wrong tool wastes incident hours—this guide maps each scenario to the right pick.

speedtest-vs-pagespeed.title: Network Probing vs PageSpeed: Reachability vs Performance Decision Order
speedtest-vs-pagespeed.hook: Page can score 100 yet be unreachable in Xinjiang. Test reachability first, performance second.

speedtest-vs-uptime.title: Active Probes vs 24/7 Monitoring: SpeedCE's Place in Ops Stack
speedtest-vs-uptime.hook: Probes are snapshots; monitoring is history—both needed, different jobs. Where SpeedCE fits in on-call.

speedce-itdog-combo.title: SpeedCE + ITDOG Golden Combo: Map Patrol and Continuous Ping Collaboration
speedce-itdog-combo.hook: SpeedCE for geographic picture; ITDOG for continuous ping curves—pair for intermittent and regional issues.

speedce-boce-combo.title: SpeedCE + BOCE Collaboration: Compliance and Blocking After Network Layer Cleared
speedce-boce-combo.hook: Network green but WeChat won't open—BOCE for blocking/ICP after SpeedCE clears reachability.

free-speedtest-tools-2026.title: 2026 Free Speed Test Tool Decision Tree: SpeedCE/ITDOG/BOCE by Scenario
free-speedtest-tools-2026.hook: No single free tool does everything—pick by scenario with this decision tree.

incident-report-speed-data.title: Speed Test Data in Incident Reports: Professional Postmortem Writing Templates
incident-report-speed-data.hook: Postmortems without probe timestamps and maps look amateur—templates for timeline + geographic impact.

on-call-first-5-minutes.title: On-Call First 5 Minutes: How to Run SpeedCE After an Alert
on-call-first-5-minutes.hook: Alert fires—first five minutes set incident trajectory. SpeedCE protocol for on-call before deeper dives.

speedce-vs-itdog.title: SpeedCE vs ITDOG Complete Comparison: Scenarios, Pros/Cons, and Pairing
speedce-vs-itdog.hook: Map-first vs table/ping-first—when to open which tab and how to combine without duplicate work.

speedce-vs-boce.title: SpeedCE vs BOCE Complete Comparison: Lightweight Maps vs Full Ops Toolkit
speedce-vs-boce.hook: BOCE does more than reachability; SpeedCE focuses on geographic HTTPS maps—know the boundary.

map-vs-table-tools.title: Map vs Table Speed Tools: Troubleshooting Efficiency Compared
map-vs-table-tools.hook: Tables excel at numbers; maps excel at geography—for regional faults, maps win minutes to resolution.

top5-free-speedtest-2026.title: 2026 Top 5 Free Speed Test Tools for Site Owners: Deep Review and Bookmark Picks
top5-free-speedtest-2026.hook: Curated stack for solo site owners—what to bookmark and when to use each in 2026.

ping-pe-use-cases.title: Ping.pe Complete Guide: Complementary Global/China Strategy with SpeedCE
ping-pe-use-cases.hook: Ping.pe for quick global ping matrix; SpeedCE for China carrier maps—complementary not redundant.

pagespeed-vs-network.title: PageSpeed Insights vs Network Probing: Division Site Owners Must Understand
pagespeed-vs-network.hook: Lighthouse scores don't replace "can users in Gansu open it?"—orthogonal metrics, both required.

monitoring-vs-probing.title: Monitoring Platforms vs Probe Tools: 24/7 Alerts and First Response
monitoring-vs-probing.hook: PagerDuty tells you something broke; SpeedCE tells you where—integrate both in runbooks.

developer-bookmark-list.title: Developer 2026 Bookmark Bar: 12 Links for 90% of Network Incidents
developer-bookmark-list.hook: Curated bookmark bar—SpeedCE first, then ITDOG, BOCE, PageSpeed, and status pages.

17ce-vs-speedce.title: 17CE vs SpeedCE: Legacy Table Tool vs Modern Map Tool in Practice
17ce-vs-speedce.hook: 17CE veterans vs SpeedCE maps—feature overlap, gaps, and migration tips for daily patrol.
